scope: Engine-light semantic interaction routing with a thin Unity Input System adapter and explicit modality/device evidence boundaries

sources.license_or_terms: Khronos OpenXR specification terms; architecture facts only, no copied implementation
sources.admitted_role: Action identity, action-set context, typed input/output and runtime binding separation
admitted_claims: Applications request semantic actions rather than querying one hardware button directly
Action sets group actions by application context and can be activated with priority
Suggested bindings are runtime-resolved hints and signal hardware tested by the application rather than universal device support
excluded_uses: Core does not copy OpenXR handles, paths, lifecycle, suppression, spaces or extension APIs
A generic or vendor interaction profile is not named-device evidence

sources.license_or_terms: Khronos OpenXR specification terms; capability facts only
sources.maintenance_evidence: Current OpenXR 1.1 interaction profiles and hand/gaze extension registry reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Modality, component capability, user-path and interaction-profile evidence boundary
admitted_claims: Interaction profiles describe physical arrangements and valid component paths separately from application action identity
Controller, hand-interaction and eye-gaze capabilities are extension/profile scoped and may be unavailable
Runtime binding choice can change according to user preference, device availability or accessibility settings
excluded_uses: No profile path becomes a universal Core modality implementation
No controller, hand or gaze support is claimed without exact runtime and device evidence

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; facts only, no copied implementation
sources.admitted_role: Unity semantic action identity and adapter lifecycle
admitted_claims: Actions separate input purpose from the device controls that perform it
Action and action-map stable IDs survive display-name changes
Same-frame action callback order is undefined and consumers must not infer asset order
excluded_uses: Unity action objects and callbacks do not enter Core
An Input Action Asset does not prove any bound device is usable

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; facts only, no copied implementation
sources.maintenance_evidence: Unity Input System 1.14.2 binding and rebinding documentation reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Multiple bindings, control schemes, non-destructive overrides and persisted binding identity
admitted_claims: One action can have multiple device bindings and composite bindings
Binding IDs can identify stored overrides independently of effective control paths
Interactions and processors belong to binding/action adaptation rather than application intent identity
excluded_uses: Core does not store Unity control paths, processors, interactions, composites or control schemes
The package does not ship a rebinding UI

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; boundary facts only
sources.maintenance_evidence: XR Interaction Toolkit 3.0 project setup, input mapping and scene requirements reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Separate future XRI adapter and tracked-interaction validation boundary
admitted_claims: XRI uses action-based input to separate logical and physical input across supported controllers
Interactor, interactable and interaction-manager behavior is a layer above semantic input actions
Tracked UI and 3D interaction require additional scene/runtime configuration
excluded_uses: The first Unity adapter does not depend on XRI or claim tracked interaction
Editor configuration does not prove headset behavior

sources.license_or_terms: Epic Developer Community documentation terms; architecture facts only, no copied implementation
sources.maintenance_evidence: Current Unreal Engine 5.8 Enhanced Input documentation reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Professional action, mapping-context, priority, trigger and modifier separation
admitted_claims: Input actions represent what a player can do and carry typed values
Mapping contexts can be added, removed and prioritized per player
Modifiers process raw values and triggers determine activation separately from action meaning
excluded_uses: No Unreal data asset, class hierarchy, macro, trigger or modifier API is copied
Unreal behavior does not prove Foundry conflict routing or Unity behavior

sources.license_or_terms: Microsoft Learn terms; facts and barrier coverage only
sources.admitted_role: Action-level remapping, cancellation and alternative input policy boundary
admitted_claims: Action-level remapping is more flexible than fixed control swapping
Prolonged, repeated, simultaneous, motion, speech and path-based input can create barriers and may need alternatives
Single-pointer activation should ordinarily support cancellation before completion and alternative digital routes for critical non-traditional input
excluded_uses: No guideline becomes an automatic compliance claim or mandatory Core feature
No device or assistive technology support is inferred

sources.license_or_terms: Apple Developer documentation terms; facts only
sources.maintenance_evidence: Current Gestures, Spatial Layout and Accessibility guidance reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Hand/gaze, standard-gesture, alternative-route and ergonomic non-universality boundary
admitted_claims: Standard semantic gestures can be expressed through touch, indirect spatial gestures or other input devices
Indirect gestures can reduce physical demand while direct and custom gestures need ergonomic testing and alternatives
Core functionality should remain accessible through more than one physical interaction where applicable
excluded_uses: Apple interaction patterns are not universal XR semantics or a Unity implementation prescription
No Vision Pro, hand, gaze, accessibility or comfort result transfers to another platform or device

source_gate_non_claims: No source grants package maturity, release, certification, compliance, comfort, platform or device authority
The first implementation is a semantic routing and Unity Input System adapter substrate, not a complete player interaction experience
Official ownership never substitutes for exact repository, consumer, runtime or device verification
